input.prompt:
I need to support three branded themes in my .NET MAUI app — a light theme,
a dark theme, and a high-contrast theme. Users should be able to pick their
preferred theme from a settings page and it should apply immediately across
the app. How do I set this up?

I need to support three branded themes in my .NET MAUI app — a light theme,

input.initialPrompt:
I need to support three branded themes in my .NET MAUI app — a light theme,
a dark theme, and a high-contrast theme. Users should be able to pick their
preferred theme from a settings page and it should apply immediately across
the app. How do I set this up?

data.content:
Here's a practical approach:

## 1. Define each theme as a `ResourceDictionary`

```xml
<!-- Resources/Themes/LightTheme.xaml -->
<ResourceDictionary xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
                    xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml">
    <Color x:Key="PageBackground">#FFFFFF</Color>
    <Color x:Key="TextPrimary">#1A1A1A</Color>
    <Color x:Key="Accent">#0078D4</Color>
</ResourceDictionary>
```

```xml
<!-- Resources/Themes/DarkTheme.xaml -->
<ResourceDictionary ...>
    <Color x:Key="PageBackground">#1E1E1E</Color>
    <Color x:Key="TextPrimary">#E0E0E0</Color>
    <Color x:Key="Accent">#4FC3F7</Color>
</ResourceDictionary>
```

```xml
<!-- Resources/Themes/HighContrastTheme.xaml -->
<ResourceDictionary ...>
    <Color x:Key="PageBackground">#000000</Color>
    <Color x:Key="TextPrimary">#FFFFFF</Color>
    <Color x:Key="Accent">#FFFF00</Color>
</ResourceDictionary>
```

## 2. Load a default theme in `App.xaml`

```xml
<Application.Resources>
    <ResourceDictionary>
        <ResourceDictionary.MergedDictionaries>
            <ResourceDictionary Source="Resources/Themes/LightTheme.xaml" />
        </ResourceDictionary.MergedDictionaries>
    </ResourceDictionary>
</Application.Resources>
```

## 3. Create a theme-switching service

```csharp
public enum AppTheme { Light, Dark, HighContrast }

public static class ThemeService
{
    public static void ApplyTheme(AppTheme theme)
    {
        var mergedDicts = Application.Current!.Resources.MergedDictionaries;
        mergedDicts.Clear();

        mergedDicts.Add(theme switch
        {
            AppTheme.Dark         => new DarkTheme(),
            AppTheme.HighContrast => new HighContrastTheme(),
            _                     => new LightTheme(),
        });

        // Persist the choice
        Preferences.Set("app_theme", theme.ToString());
    }

    public static void LoadSavedTheme()
    {
        if (Enum.TryParse<AppTheme>(Preferences.Get("app_theme", "Light"), out var saved))
            ApplyTheme(saved);
    }
}
```

> The `new DarkTheme()` calls work because each `.xaml` file with a code-behind compiles to a class. Make sure each theme XAML has an `x:Class` (e.g., `x:Class="MyApp.Resources.Themes.DarkTheme"`).

## 4. Call `LoadSavedTheme()` on startup

```csharp
// App.xaml.cs
public App()
{
    InitializeComponent();
    ThemeService.LoadSavedTheme();
}
```

## 5. Wire up the settings page

```xml
<!-- SettingsPage.xaml -->
<Picker Title="Theme" SelectedIndexChanged="OnThemeChanged">
    <Picker.ItemsSource>
        <x:Array Type="{x:Type x:String}">
            <x:String>Light</x:String>
            <x:String>Dark</x:String>
            <x:String>HighContrast</x:String>
        </x:Array>
    </Picker.ItemsSource>
</Picker>
```

```csharp
void OnThemeChanged(object sender, EventArgs e)
{
    if (sender is Picker p && Enum.TryParse<AppTheme>(p.SelectedItem?.ToString(), out var theme))
        ThemeService.ApplyTheme(theme);
}
```

## 6. Consume theme colors in any page

```xml
<Label Text="Hello" TextColor="{DynamicResource TextPrimary}" />
<ContentPage BackgroundColor="{DynamicResource PageBackground}">
```

**Key point:** use `{DynamicResource}`, not `{StaticResource}`, so controls pick up the new values immediately when the dictionary is swapped at runtime.
data.reasoningText: The user is asking for guidance on how to set up themed resource dictionaries in a .NET MAUI app with light, dark, and high-contrast themes, switchable at runtime from a settings page. This is a general "how-to" question, not a codebase task. I'll provide a concise but complete answer.
